title: polypodium dryopteris subsp. robertianum
intro: Polypodium dryopteris subsp. robertianum, also known as Polypodium vulgare subsp. robertianum, is a subspecies of the Polypodium vulgare fern, native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Polypodium dryopteris subsp. robertianum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antispasmodic agent to alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Polypodium dryopteris subsp. robertianum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
